variantRecordData.name: SpatialVariantStruct
variantRecordData.semantics: Variant Record for a single spatial attribute.

alternative.name: SpatialStatic
alternative.semantics: Variant for representing a static object.

alternative.name: SpatialFPW
alternative.semantics: Variant for representing an object with a constant velocity (or low acceleration) linear motion. With the linear velocity provided in reference system coordinates (world or host entity's coordinate system).

alternative.name: SpatialRPW
alternative.semantics: Variant for representing an object with a constant velocity (or low acceleration) linear motion, including rotation information. With the linear velocity provided in reference system coordinates (world or host entity's coordinate system).

alternative.name: SpatialRVW
alternative.semantics: Variant for representing an object with high speed or maneuvering at any speed, including rotation information. With the linear velocity and acceleration provided in reference system coordinates (world or host entity's coordinate system).

alternative.name: SpatialFVW
alternative.semantics: Variant for representing an object with high speed or maneuvering at any speed. With the linear velocity and acceleration provided in reference system coordinates (world or host entity's coordinate system).

alternative.name: SpatialFPB
alternative.semantics: Variant for representing an object with a constant velocity (or low acceleration) linear motion. With the linear velocity provided in own body axis coordinates.

alternative.name: SpatialRPB
alternative.semantics: Variant for representing an object with a constant velocity (or low acceleration) linear motion, including rotation information. With the linear velocity provided in own body axis coordinates.

alternative.name: SpatialRVB
alternative.semantics: Variant for representing an object with high speed or maneuvering at any speed, including rotation information. With the linear velocity and acceleration provided in own body axis coordinates.

alternative.name: SpatialFVB
alternative.semantics: Variant for representing an object with high speed or maneuvering at any speed. With the linear velocity and acceleration provided in own body axis coordinates.

variantRecordData.name: StationNameLocationVariantStruct
variantRecordData.semantics: The station name at which the constituent part is located. In case of 'On Station', the alternative specifies its location relative to the host object.

alternative.name: RelativeLocation
alternative.semantics: The location of the constituent part object relative to the host object entity coordinate system.

alternative.name: RelativeRangeAndBearing
alternative.semantics: The location of the constituent part object relative to the host object in polar coordinates.

variantRecordData.name: TransponderDeltaAltitudeVariantStruct
variantRecordData.semantics: Delta to the transponder reported altitude represented as a 11-bit binary coded altitude.

alternative.name: DeltaAltitude100Foot
alternative.semantics: Delta altitude in 100-foot increments.

alternative.name: DeltaAltitude25Foot
alternative.semantics: Delta altitude in 25-foot increments.

variantRecordData.name: TransponderRelativeAltitudeVariantStruct
variantRecordData.semantics: Transponder reported altitude represented as a 11-bit binary coded altitude referenced to -1000 feet. All altitudes greater than 50175 feet shall be reported with 100-foot resolution. When no altitude is reported, the alternative value shall be set to 2047 (decimal) to indicate to use entity truth altitude to derive the transponder reported altitude.

alternative.name: RelativeAltitude100Foot
alternative.semantics: Relative altitude in 100-foot increments.

alternative.name: RelativeAltitude25Foot
alternative.semantics: Relative altitude in 25-foot increments.

variantRecordData.name: VariableParameterVariantStruct
variantRecordData.semantics:
Variant record specifying information on articulated parts, attached parts, and other data associated with an entity or detonation. Based on the Variable Parameter record as specified in IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Section 6.2.94.
Note that usage of this datatype for the PhysicalEntity object class and MunitionDetonation interaction class attribute/parameter VariableParametersArray shall be in accordance with IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Annex I.

alternative.name: ArticulatedPart
alternative.semantics: Alternative for an articulated part.

alternative.name: AttachedPart
alternative.semantics: Alternative for an attached part.

alternative.name: Separation
alternative.semantics: Alternative for a separation.

alternative.name: EntityType
alternative.semantics: Alternative for an entity type.

alternative.name: EntityAssociation
alternative.semantics: Alternative for an entity association.

note.name: MOM1
note.semantics: The value of the Dimension Upper Bound entry for the Federate dimension is RTI implementation dependent.

note.name: RPRnoteAggregate4
note.semantics: The units and semantics for the MarkingData array elements are specified by the value of the MarkingEncodingType.

note.name: RPRnoteAggregate5
note.semantics: The interpretation of the 32 bits defining the entity appearance is to be derived from the federation agreements, which could refer to the entity appearance record as defined in SISO-REF-010. The reason that this has not been split out into separate fields (as has been done for the subclasses of BaseEntity) is the difficulty of providing an efficient manner of defining an array of such appearance fields.

note.name: RPRnoteBase1
note.semantics: Federates shall send the time at which the data is valid in the user-supplied tag with every attribute values update and interaction; see datatype RPRUserDefinedTag for the encoding. All federates shall transmit this field, even if they do not use it themselves, so that other federates can use its value to compensate for network transport delays.

note.name: RPRnoteBase4
note.semantics: If the entity is a constituent part of another entity (denoted by the IsPartOf attribute being set appropriately) then the Spatial attribute may be ignored by a receiving federate. Instead, the receiving federate can calculate spatial attribute values by adding the offsets provided in the RelativeSpatial attribute to the values provided in the host entity's Spatial attribute. Even if a federate is updating RelativeSpatial, it should still update Spatial for the benefit of federates who do not subscribe to the optional RelativeSpatial and IsPartOf attributes.

note.name: RPRnoteBase5
note.semantics: The update condition shall be evaluated as follows: The owner of a base entity object shall maintain two state models of the object in support of the dead reckoning process. One model shall be the internal model used by the simulation application to represent that object. The other shall be a dead reckoning model of the object. Certain thresholds shall be established as criteria for determining if the object's actual TSPI data has varied by an allowable amount from the dead reckoned TSPI data. The update condition is TRUE when either: a) the object's actual position differs from the dead reckoned position by more than DRA_POS_THRSH (default value: 1 m), or b) the object's actual orientation differs from the dead reckoned orientation by more than DRA_ORIENT_THRSH (default value: 3 degree). See IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Sections 4.6.6 and 5.3.2.3 and Annex E for more details on dead reckoning.

note.name: RPRnoteBase6
note.semantics: This must reference a valid Object instance.

note.name: RPRnoteBase7
note.semantics: The units of the Value field depends on the value of the ParameterTypeMetric field. The units are defined in IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Section I.2.2.5.

note.name: RPRnoteBase8
note.semantics: All fields in the entity type struct are enumerations. The values for the individual fields are to be derived from the federation agreements, which could refer to SISO-REF-010. The values used in this structure should comply with the requirements specified in IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Section 6.2.30 (for platform and environmental entities) and Section 6.2.5 (for aggregate entities).

note.name: RPRnoteBase9
note.semantics: Frozen entities should not be dead-reckoned, i.e. should be displayed as fixed at the current location even if non-zero velocity, acceleration or rotation data received from the frozen entity.

note.name: RPRnoteBase10
note.semantics: If the entity is a constituent part of another entity (denoted by the IsPartOf attribute being set appropriately) then the IsFrozen attribute is no longer updated. The frozen status of the entity is the same as the frozen status of the host entity.

note.name: RPRnoteBase11
note.semantics: The DatumLength equals the length in bits of the DatumValue only. The total size of a VariableDatumStruct record must account for the padding length.

note.name: RPRnoteBase12
note.semantics: The type of the DatumValue field is determined by the value of the DatumID field. The types and associated units, etc., for each of the DatumID enumeration values are to be derived from the federation agreements, which could refer to SISO-REF-010. The DatumValue element type is defined as a UnsignedInteger64 (64 bits) to ensure the correct byte alignment for types that include 64-bit elements.

note.name: RPRnoteBase13
note.semantics: This note applies when this datatype is used within the BaseEntity.IsPartOf attribute. The following StationName enumerations - On station RNG/BRG (15) and On station - x,y,z (16), are optional to transmit along with associated RelativeLocation or RelativeRangeAndBearing information. If these enumeration values (15) and (16) are received, they shall be ignored. The RelativeSpatial attribute shall be used in all cases. (Note: The RelativeLocation field uses the same Location contained in the RelativeSpatial attribute. In both these cases, the values represent the relative location of a part entity to the host entity in the referenced entity coordinate system. The RelativeRangeAndBearing field does not provide full relative spatial data and, therefore, cannot be substituted for the RelativeSpatial attribute.)

note.name: RPRnoteCommunication1
note.semantics: This attribute is updated if the current value differs from the previously updated value by more than the value specified by the symbolic name (default value: 500m).

note.name: RPRnoteCommunication2
note.semantics: This must reference a valid Object instance.

note.name: RPRnoteCommunication3
note.semantics: All the fields in the radio type struct are enumerations. The values for the individual fields (that do not use enumeration datatypes from within the RPR FOM) are to be derived from the federation agreements, which could refer to SISO-REF-010.

note.name: RPRnoteDER1
note.semantics: If there is no object instance associated with the attribute, then this should be set to the empty string (no characters). Refer to SISO-STD-001 Section 7.8.6 for handling empty strings.

note.name: RPRnoteDER2
note.semantics:
The update condition shall be evaluated as follows: The owner of a designator object shall maintain two state models of the object in support of the dead reckoning process. One model shall be the internal model used by the simulation application to represent that object. The other shall be a dead reckoning model of the object. Certain thresholds shall be established as criteria for determining if the object's actual spot location has varied by an allowable amount from the dead reckoned spot location. The attribute is updated when the object's actual spot location differs from the dead reckoned spot location by more than DRA_POS_THRSH (default value: 1 m). See IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Sections 4.6.6 and 5.3.2.3 and Annex E for more details.
A TSPI update includes all attributes applicable to the dead reckoning algorithm. A TSPI update attribute may be omitted if its last updated value is within an established epsilon amount from its current value.

note.name: RPRnoteDER3
note.semantics: The epsilon value for the location is DRA_POS_EPSILSON (default value: 0.001 m), in any direction.

note.name: RPRnoteDER4
note.semantics: The epsilon value for the linear acceleration is DRA_ACCEL_EPSILSON (default value: 0.001 m/(s^2)), in any direction.

note.name: RPRnoteDER5
note.semantics: The EventIdentifier attribute is used to link together updates arising from a common event. For example an emitter function change can be linked to a frequency change in one of its associated emitter beams, by using the same event ID in the attribute updates to the appropriate EmitterSystem and EmitterBeam classes. It is important to note that in order that the EventIdentifier is delivered in the same reflect attribute values call in the receiving federates as the associated attributes, then all the attributes, including the EventIdentifier, must share the same transport type.

note.name: RPRnoteDER6
note.semantics: The federate should change the value of the EventIdentifier when associating changes between two or more emitter beams and/or emitter systems. It is not necessary to update the EventIdentifier field with every attribute update if the change is not associated with changes in other instances of other classes.

note.name: RPRnoteDER7
note.semantics: If emissions data is available (determined by the state of the EmissionsDataAvailable attribute) then this attribute shall be updated by the federate. If emissions data is not available then this attribute shall not be updated by the federate.

note.name: RPRnoteDER8
note.semantics: This attribute is updated if the current value differs from the previously updated value by more than the value specified by the symbolic name (default value: 3 degrees).

note.name: RPRnoteDER9
note.semantics: This attribute is updated if the current value differs from the previously updated value by more than the value specified by the symbolic name (default value: 3 degrees).

note.name: RPRnoteDER10
note.semantics: A change in sweep sync value alone does not require an update to be published. When one of the other beam attributes (center and sweep for azimuth and elevation ) requires an update publication, the sweep sync attribute shall be included when its value has changed since the last update.

note.name: RPRnoteDER11
note.semantics: This attribute is updated if the current value differs from the previously updated value by more than the value specified by the symbolic name (default value: 1 degree).

note.name: RPRnoteDER12
note.semantics: This attribute is updated if the current value differs from the previously updated value by more than the value specified by the symbolic name (default value: 1 degree).

note.name: RPRnoteDER13
note.semantics: The term "ERP" is widely used throughout the radar community (including radar databases), but in radar contexts, the intended meaning is actually that of EIRP.

note.name: RPRnoteDER14
note.semantics: This must reference a valid Object instance.

note.name: RPRnoteDER15
note.semantics: The object IDs in this structure shall refer to PhysicalEntity objects (or subclasses of PhysicalEntity).

note.name: RPRnoteDER16
note.semantics: Special update conditions may apply to the datatypes or their fields used for the array elements. Refer to the datatype or field semantics, including any referenced notes, for more details.

note.name: RPRnoteDER17
note.semantics: The object IDs in this structure shall refer to EmitterBeam objects (or subclasses of EmitterBeam).

note.name: RPRnoteDER18
note.semantics: If the IFF system is Mode 5 capable then this attribute shall be updated by the federate. If the IFF system is not Mode 5 capable then this attribute shall either not be published or set to False.

note.name: RPRnoteDER19
note.semantics: The RRB system is a transponder only system.

note.name: RPRnoteDER20
note.semantics: Upon a value change, an update is only required to be published immediately when the change exceeds a threshold value. For azimuth related data, the threshold is defined by the symbolic name EE_AZ_THRSH (default value: 1 degree). For elevation related data, the threshold is defined by the symbolic name EE_EL_THRSH (default value: 1 degree).

note.name: RPRnoteDER21
note.semantics: Upon a value change, an update is only required to be published immediately when the change exceeds a threshold value. The threshold is defined by the symbolic name EE_AD_PULRAT_THRSH (default value: 0.017 rad/s).

note.name: RPRnoteDER22
note.semantics: Upon a value change, an update is only required to be published immediately when the change exceeds a threshold value. The threshold is defined by the symbolic name EE_AD_PULACC_THRSH (default value: 0.017 rad/(s^2)).

note.name: RPRnoteDER23
note.semantics: Upon a value change, an update is only required to be published immediately when the change exceeds a threshold value. The threshold is defined by the symbolic name EE_FT_VEL_THRSH (default value: 1.0 m/s).

note.name: RPRnoteDER24
note.semantics: Upon a value change, an update is only required to be published immediately when the change exceeds a threshold value. The threshold is defined by the symbolic name EE_FT_ACC_THRSH (default value: 1.0 m/(s^2)).

note.name: RPRnoteDER25
note.semantics: Upon a value change, an update is only required to be published immediately when the change exceeds a threshold value. The threshold is defined by the symbolic name EE_FT_MWD_THRSH (default value: 10000 m).

note.name: RPRnoteDER26
note.semantics: Upon a value change, an update is only required to be published immediately when the change exceeds a threshold value. The threshold is defined by the symbolic name EE_FT_KT_THRSH (default value: 10 s).

note.name: RPRnoteDER27
note.semantics: Upon a value change, an update is only required to be published immediately when the change exceeds a threshold value. The threshold is defined by the symbolic name EE_FT_ESP_THRSH (default value: 10 m).

note.name: RPRnoteDER28
note.semantics: For radar-like IFF interrogators, this may still be known as 'ERP' because EIRP is generally known throughout the radar community as ERP. The field is named "ERP" in IEEE Std 1278.1-2012, but it is believed that EIRP was the intended meaning.

note.name: RPRnoteDER29
note.semantics: Frequency modulation shall be derived from database parameters stored for the particular emitting system and specified mode.

note.name: RPRnoteDER30
note.semantics: IFF Data records have not been defined for specific DAP data. When such records are developed, it is recommended that the DAP field format from the appropriate ICAO publication be used.

note.name: RPRnoteDER31
note.semantics: The size of this field in IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Section B.2.51 is assumed to be incorrect. According to ICAO documentation, this field in the extended squitter surface position BDS register is 7 bits, not 3. RPR FOM users should be careful in using the full range as per the ICAO specifications. For in heterogeneous exercises with DIS-based simulations this may lead to unexpected results. It is advised to explicitly note the allowed values and the mapping to be applied in gateways in federation/exercise agreements (when using Mode S squitter surface position reports).

note.name: RPRnoteDER32
note.semantics: At the time of publication of the Standard for RPR FOM 3.0, no IFF Data records have been defined for specific DAP data.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations1
note.semantics: The gaps in enumeration values are intentional and are reserved for future additions.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations2
note.semantics: Surface includes a water platform surface such as a ship deck

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations3
note.semantics: Nose and Left/Right Main move in unison; intended for low-fidelity simulations and not to be used if the others are used.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations4
note.semantics: Point defense systems like Phalanx guns, or visible chaff, flare, or smoke dispensers.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations5
note.semantics: Any radar dish or movable antenna or sensor.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations6
note.semantics: Masts are numbered from forward to aft, masts that are side by side are numbered from port to starboard

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations7

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations8

note.semantics: Called Pilots Clearview window in C-17 flight manual

note.semantics: Use United States Minor Outlying Islands

note.semantics: Includes CIS and USSR era types

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations24
note.semantics: Now Democratic Republic of the Congo

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations26
note.semantics: Not to be confused with the neighbouring Republic of the Congo

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations27
note.semantics: UID table only for historical purposes. Do not add new entries

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations28
note.semantics: There is a requirement for this variant to have an ID separate from the FL 1800.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations31
note.semantics: Add SHMEL National Nomenclature for clarity

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations33
note.semantics: Values 1-999 are reserved for United States IO Simulation Sources – See applicable agreement of the organizers of the event (training, exercise, etc.) in which information operations is included.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations34
note.semantics: The 1's digit is reserved for Race/Ethnicity and the enumerations are taken from the U.S. OMB. The 10's digit is reserved for general age group. The 100's digit is reserved for gender, where 0 is Male and 1 is Female.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations36
note.semantics: Subcategories 150-199 are restricted to non-weapon equipment classes.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations37
note.semantics: Subcategories 151-159 are restricted to sensor equipment classes.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations38
note.semantics: Subcategories 160-169 are restricted to animal companion classes.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations40

note.semantics: Subcategories for Surface Platform Category 101. For Subcategory 1-3. Use the Extra field to specify the capacity 1-255 persons.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations49
note.semantics: Where 7xxy = xx * 10^y equivalent tons of TNT, example: 7126 = 12 * 10^6 tons of TNT or 12 Megatons of TNT.

note.semantics: SATURN was formerly HAVE QUICK IIA (HQIIA)

note.semantics: No specific enumerations may be placed in this subcategory

At least two additional types of CVSD, known as CCTT CVSD and CECOM CVSD, also use Enumeration value 2. Make sure that if you are using CVSD that your system is compatible with other systems playing in an exercise.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerations164

note.semantics: Not used, as it is described in Munitions.

note.semantics: The variable-datum-value field consists of:32-bit unsigned integer, Data Collection Type; 32-bit unused; and multiple of 64 bits, SIMNET Data Collection Variant.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerationsBitSize01
note.semantics: WARNING: The enumeration is defined in SISO-REF-010 with a size of 1 bit. If interoperability with DIS-based simulation applications is important, limit the value range to 0-1 when modifying this enumeration.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerationsBitSize02
note.semantics: WARNING: The enumeration is defined in SISO-REF-010 with a size of 2 bits. If interoperability with DIS-based simulation applications is important, limit the value range to 0-3 when modifying this enumeration.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerationsBitSize03
note.semantics: WARNING: The enumeration is defined in SISO-REF-010 with a size of 3 bits. If interoperability with DIS-based simulation applications is important, limit the value range to 0-7 when modifying this enumeration.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerationsBitSize04
note.semantics: WARNING: The enumeration is defined in SISO-REF-010 with a size of 4 bits. If interoperability with DIS-based simulation applications is important, limit the value range to 0-15 when modifying this enumeration.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerationsBitSize05
note.semantics: WARNING: The enumeration is defined in SISO-REF-010 with a size of 5 bits. If interoperability with DIS-based simulation applications is important, limit the value range to 0-31 when modifying this enumeration.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerationsBitSize06
note.semantics: WARNING: The enumeration is defined in SISO-REF-010 with a size of 6 bits. If interoperability with DIS-based simulation applications is important, limit the value range to 0-63 when modifying this enumeration.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerationsBitSize07
note.semantics: WARNING: The enumeration is defined in SISO-REF-010 with a size of 7 bits. If interoperability with DIS-based simulation applications is important, limit the value range to 0-127 when modifying this enumeration.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerationsBitSize12
note.semantics: WARNING: The enumeration is defined in SISO-REF-010 with a size of 12 bits. If interoperability with DIS-based simulation applications is important, limit the value range to 0-4095 when modifying this enumeration.

note.name: RPRnoteEnumerationsBitSize14
note.semantics: WARNING: The enumeration is defined in SISO-REF-010 with a size of 14 bits. If interoperability with DIS-based simulation applications is important, limit the value range to 0-16383 when modifying this enumeration.

note.name: RPRnoteFoundation1
note.semantics:
This is the unique ObjectName associated with each object instance. The user can define the name to be used in the registerObjectInstance RTI call. If user does not define the name then the RTI will generate a unique name for the object. RTI generated names may be fairly long, so federations wishing to conserve bandwidth may wish to implement their own object naming scheme.
The ObjectName is provided by the RTI in the discoverObjectInstance call. The user can also obtain the ObjectName for a particular object instance using the getObjectInstanceName call.

note.name: RPRnoteLogistics1
note.semantics: This must reference a valid Object instance.

note.name: RPRnoteMinefield7
note.semantics: Default value: Minefield Location (not needed for single mines)

note.name: RPRnoteMinefield11
note.semantics: Required if tripwires are used, otherwise the default value is Empty.

note.name: RPRnoteMinefield12
note.semantics: Required if the sum of values in NumberTripDetonationWires is greater than 0, otherwise the default value is Empty.

note.name: RPRnoteMinefield13
note.semantics: Required if the sum of values in NumberWireVertices is greater than 0, otherwise the default values is Empty.

note.name: RPRnotePhysical13
note.semantics: For the Air Domain named 'Propulsion Killed' in SISO-REF-010.

note.name: RPRnotePhysical25
note.semantics: For the Land Domain named 'Trailing Dust Cloud' and for the Surface Domain named 'Wake Size' in SISO-REF-010.

note.name: RPRnotePhysical30
note.semantics: For the Surface Domain named 'Spot Lights On' in SISO-REF-010.

note.name: RPRnotePhysical31
note.semantics: For the Land Domain named 'Interior Lights (Forward) On' in SISO-REF-010.

note.name: RPRnotePhysical32
note.semantics: For the Land Domain named 'Primary Hatch' in SISO-REF-010. If the entity can operate beneath the surface of the sea, the valid values may need to be limited to 0, 1, and 4, in accordance with AppearanceSubsurfaceHatchEnum8.

note.name: RPRnotePhysical58
note.semantics: If there is no object instance associated with the parameter, then this should be set to the empty string (no characters). Refer to SISO-STD-001 Section 7.8.6 for handling empty strings.

note.name: RPRnotePhysical59
note.semantics: This must reference a valid object instance.

note.name: RPRnotePhysical60
note.semantics: If the entity does not model or otherwise cannot provide this information, a value of -273.15 °C shall be used.

note.name: RPRnotePhysical61
note.semantics: If no fire or detonation event is applicable, this field shall be set to all zeroes.

note.name: RPRnotePhysical62
note.semantics: The units and semantics for the MarkingData array elements are specified by the value of the MarkingEncodingType.

note.name: RPRnoteSE15
note.semantics: For interoperability with DIS, according to SISO-REF-010, limit the value range to 0 to 63 (6 bits).

note.name: RPRnoteSE23
note.semantics: The structure of this datatype follows that defined in SISO-REF-010 (Section 12.3.5.1).

note.name: RPRnoteSE26
note.semantics: There is no data associated with a uniform geometry record. However, to enable inclusion as an alternative in the EnvironmentRecVariantStruct, the fixed record has been modeled according to [UID 266].

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN1
note.semantics: The Acknowledge interaction is issued in response to the CreateEntity, RemoveEntity, StartResume, and StopFreeze interactions. See IEEE Std 1278.1-20128 Sections 5.6.5.6 and 7.5.6.

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN2
note.semantics: The AcknowledgeR interaction is issued in response to the CreateEntityR, RemoveEntityR, StartResumeR, and StopFreezeR interactions. See IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Sections 5.12.4.6 and 7.11.6.

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN3
note.semantics: The ActionResponse interaction should be sent in response to an ActionRequest interaction by the receiving federate.

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN4
note.semantics: The ActionResponseR interaction should be sent in response to an ActionRequestR interaction by the receiving federate.

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN5
note.semantics: See IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Section 5.12 for a description of the acknowledgement protocols (called reliability services in that document).

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN6
note.semantics: If the EntityNumber field is set to RQST_ASSIGN_ID (fixed value: FFFE (H)) then the receiving application should assign the entity number. Entity identifications shall be in accordance with IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Section 6.2.28

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN7
note.semantics: The Data interaction should be sent in response to a DataQuery interaction by the receiving federate.

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN8
note.semantics: The DataR interaction should be sent in response to a DataQueryR interaction by the receiving federate.

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN9
note.semantics: The Data interaction should be sent in response to a SetData interaction by the receiving federate.

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN10
note.semantics: The DataR interaction should be sent in response to a SetDataR interaction by the receiving federate.

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN11
note.semantics: The RecordR interaction should be sent in response to a RecordQueryR interaction by the receiving federate.

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN12
note.semantics: The RecordR interaction should be sent in response to a SetRecordR interaction by the receiving federate.

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN13
note.semantics:
The AttributeChangeResult interaction should be sent in response to an AttributeChangeRequest interaction by any object that is mentioned in the associated object list. The AttributeChangeResult parameter should indicate the success or failure of the object to implement the requested changes. If the object can implement the changes then the AttributeChangeResult parameter should be set to successful and the AttributeValueSet shall be empty.
If the object cannot immediately comply with the request, but is likely to be able to comply in the future then the AttributeChangeRequest parameter shall be set to pending and the AttributeValueSet shall be empty. When the object complies with the request (or fails to implement the request) then another AttributeChangeResult interaction shall be sent informing the initiating federate of the result of the request.
If the object cannot comply with the request then the AttributeChangeResult parameter shall indicate the reason why the object cannot comply with the request. The AttributeValueSet shall contain the attributes which have been not been set and the current values of those attributes.

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN14
note.semantics:
The AttributeChangeResultR interaction should be sent in response to an AttributeChangeRequestR interaction by any object that is mentioned in the associated object list. The AttributeChangeResult parameter should indicate the success or failure of the object to implement the requested changes. If the object can implement the changes then the AttributeChangeResult parameter should be set to successful and the AttributeValueSet shall be empty.
If the object cannot immediately comply with the request, but is likely to be able to comply in the future then the AttributeChangeRequest parameter shall be set to pending and the AttributeValueSet shall be empty. When the object complies with the request (or fails to implement the request) then another AttributeChangeResultR interaction shall be sent informing the initiating federate of the result of the request.
If the object cannot comply with the request then the AttributeChangeResult parameter shall indicate the reason why the object cannot comply with the request. The AttributeValueSet shall contain the attributes which have been not been set and the current values of those attributes.

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN15
note.semantics: The CreateObjectResult interaction should be sent in response to an CreateObjectRequest interaction by the receiving federate.

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN16
note.semantics: The CreateObjectResultR interaction should be sent in response to an CreateObjectRequestR interaction by the receiving federate.

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN17
note.semantics: The RemoveObjectResult interaction should be sent in response to an RemoveObjectRequest interaction by the receiving federate.

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN18
note.semantics: The standard acknowledgement protocol is identical to the requirements of SIMAN in IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Section 5.6 (i.e. it acts identically to the parent interaction). The other acknowledgement protocols are described in IEEE Std 1278.1-2012 Section 5.12 (they are called reliability services in that document).

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN19
note.semantics: The ActionResponseFromObject interaction should be sent in response to an ActionRequestToObject interaction by the receiving federate.

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN20
note.semantics: The ActionResponseFromObjectR interaction should be sent in response to an ActionRequestToObjectR interaction by the receiving federate.

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN21
note.semantics: The units of the FixedDatumValue field is determined by the value of the FixedDatumIdentifier field. The units, etc., for each of the FixedDatumIdentifier enumeration values are to be derived from the federation agreements, which could refer to SISO-REF-010.

note.name: RPRnoteSIMAN22
note.semantics: The type of the RecordStruct is determined by the value of the RecordSetIdentifier field. The types and associated units, etc., for each of the RecordSetIdentifier enumeration values The values for the individual fields are to be derived from the federation agreements, which could refer to SISO-REF-010.

note.name: RPRnoteWarfare1
note.semantics: This must reference a valid Object instance.

note.name: RPRnoteWarfare2
note.semantics: Refer to SISO-STD-001 Section 7.13.1.1 for a detailed description of the update conditions of each of the alternatives possible as array element.

note.name: RPRnoteWarfare3
note.semantics: If there is no object instance associated with the parameter, then this should be set to the empty string (no characters). Refer to SISO-STD-001 Section 7.8.6 for handling empty strings.
